title: Fluffy Greek Lamb Lahana Moussaka
description: A creative twist on the classic Greek moussaka, with crispy fried eggplant and a rich, creamy bechamel sauce.
instructions: Cut the eggplants into 1/2-inch thick slices and salt them to draw out excess moisture.
Let the eggplant slices sit for 30 minutes, then rinse and pat them dry.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat and fry the eggplant slices until crispy and golden, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Transfer the fried eggplant to a plate and set aside.
In the same skillet, cook the lamb shoulder over medium-high heat until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the onion and garlic to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the tomato puree and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Arrange a layer of fried eggplant in the bottom of a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Spread half of the lamb and tomato mixture over the eggplant, followed by half of the bechamel sauce.
Sprinkle with half of the pecorino cheese.
Repeat the layers, starting with the eggplant, then the remaining lamb and tomato mixture, the remaining bechamel sauce, and finally the remaining pecorino cheese.
Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for 35-40 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Sprinkle with parsley and serve hot.
